title: What's New in the Documentation for this Patch (10.4.03.041)
breadcrumbs: What's New in the Documentation for this Patch (10.4.03.041)
text: What's New in the Documentation for this Patch (10.4.03.041) The Uniface 10 documentation is regularly updated to reflect new features, enhancements, bug fixes, and user feedback. Significant documentation changes are listed here. For the complete list of new software features, bug fixes, and platform support updates, see Rocket Community: Downloads . 10.4 .03.041 Feature: Appearance Property for ux-Layout Widgets A new 'appearance' property has been added to the ux-Layout widgets, such as uxEntLayout and uxCompLayout, allowing developers to control the visual style of these layout widgets. For more information, see uxcomplayout and uxentlayout . Feature: Addition of resize auto value for ux-TextArea The ux-TextArea widget now supports the resize: 'auto' property, enabling flexible resizing behavior based on the layout context. For more information, see uxtextarea . Feature: New Field-level Widgets Uniface UX has been enhanced with new field‑level widgets, ux-DateTimePicker and ux-TimePicker . Additionally, ux-TextField now supports the html:type property with the values 'time' and 'datetime-local'. For more information, see ux-DateTimePicker , ux-TimePicker , and uxtextfield . 10.4 .03.040 JavaScript API Deprecation Warnings Uniface now detects the use of deprecated JavaScript API functions and properties at runtime and automatically reports warning messages in the browser’s developer console. These warnings help developers identify deprecated APIs early and migrate to supported alternatives before support is removed in a future release. For more information, see JavaScript API Deprecation Warnings . 10.4 .03.039 Enhancement: Field Templates Updated to Use UX Widgets Field templates now use UX widgets by default. For more information, see Define a Modeled Entity . Doc update: Guidelines to handle Error -262 A new guideline to handle the procerror -262 (UWEBERR_DSP_PROTOCOL) is added. The procerror -262 might appear after migrating to Uniface 10.4.02 or later. For more information, see Handling Error -262 After Migrating to Uniface 10.4.02 or Later and $procerror . 10.4 .03.038 Feature: UX Component‑Level Widgets This release adds support for UX component level widgets in Uniface DSP applications. Component widgets allow UX behavior to be defined at the component level and operate alongside existing entity and field level UX widgets. Component widgets: Are treated as a top class UX widget type Enable reusable UX behavior at the component level Can be used to bootstrap the UX of a DSP without requiring a Design Layout IDE support has been extended to allow UX component widget types and properties to be defined and maintained. For more information, see Widget Reference: Dynamic Server Pages , Uniface UX , and UX Widgets . Feature: Layout Mode Property A new Layout Mode component property controls how DSP layout content is processed during HTML generation. The supported layout modes are Full Document, Generated, and Fragment. The Layout Mode property allows UX component widgets to be used in DSPs without requiring a Design Layout, while preserving backward compatibility. For more information, see Layout Template System , Layout Mode Property , and Customizing Layout Template . Feature: ProcScript Functions for Component-Level Widgets Two new ProcScript functions provide property control for UX component widgets: $componentproperties — Gets or sets definition-time component widget properties $instanceproperties — Gets or sets runtime instance properties for component widgets These functions align component widget property handling with the existing property handling used for entity and field widgets. For more information, see $componentproperties and $instanceproperties . Feature: New Layout widgets Uniface UX now includes three new layout widgets that streamline the construction of page, component, and entity layouts: uxHeaderFooter (page level) : Provides a header–main–footer structure with intelligent slot distribution and sticky positioning. For more information, see ux-HeaderFooter and uxheaderfooter . uxCompLayout (component level) : Supports organized component‑level layouts with labels and flexible alignment. For more information, see ux-Layout and uxcomplayout . uxEntLayout (entity level) : Offers layout options for collections and occurrences with configurable arrangement and alignment. For more information, see ux-Layout and uxentlayout . Feature: New 'direct' argument added for $PUTMESS_LOG_FILE and $TRANSCRIPT_LOG_FILE A new 'direct' argument is added for the logging configuration in assignment files. It is for writing log messages directly to the output instead of using its internal log‑queue. For more information, see $PUTMESS_LOG_FILE and $TRANSCRIPT_LOG_FILE . 10.4 .03.032 Feature: DSP Widget Properties Dialog Enhancement Checkboxes are added to the DSP widget properties dialog to indicate configured properties. Select or clear checkboxes to add or remove properties. For more information, see Setting DSP Widget Properties . Documentation Enhancement: Updated Community Forum links Legacy Rocket Community Forum URLs have been replaced with links to the new community platform. The updated links provide improved access to resources for connecting, learning, and sharing Rocket-related content. You can find the Rocket Community Forum here: https://community.rocketsoftware.com/ . 10.4 .03.030 Feature: Observability capabilities added in Uniface for Windows and Linux platforms Uniface now introduces observability capabilities on Windows and Linux platforms. The observability feature enables you to monitor application performance, troubleshoot issues, and optimize reliability without modifying the existing code. For more information, see Observability , Observability Setup , [OBSERVABILITY] , and Testing Environment Setup Examples . 10.4 .03.028 Documentation Enhancement: Visual Previews for DSP Widgets Added Added preview images of all available DSP widgets. This allows users to visually assess each widget’s design and functionality at a glance before using a widget. For more information, see Widget Reference: Dynamic Server Pages . 10.4 .03.027 Feature: Safe 3GL Component Debugging Capability – For use in development environments only The $ENABLE_USER_3GL_DEBUGGING setting allows you to debug your own 3GL components using a 3GL debugger. When $ENABLE_USER_3GL_DEBUGGING is set to TRUE , you can attach a 3GL debugger to an already running instance of Uniface. This helps in debugging your own 3GL components and avoids any interference to some of the operations Uniface performs. This applies both to 3GL components that run using the activate statement and those run using the perform statement. For more information, see $ENABLE_USER_3GL_DEBUGGING , Pathscrambler , C, C++, and Other 3GLs , 3GL Development Issues , [SETTINGS] , and Uniface and 3GL . 10.4 .03.026 Feature: Go to a line number in code You can jump to a specific line number in the code editor in the Uniface IDE using the IDE code editor popup menu. You need to right-click and click Go To on the popup menu to open the Go To pop-up box. The Go To line number option is restricted to the code of the current object. For more information, see Go to a Line Number in Code . 10.4 .03.021 Feature: -seed option for the pathscrambler The pathscrambler can now base the encryptions it does on a key that is derived from a user-defined seed. This enhances the application's security. For more information, see Encrypting Paths and Other Sensitive Data Using PathScrambler and pathscrambler.exe . 10.4 .03.019 Feature: IN parameters for triggers and webtriggers Uniface UX widgets support IN parameters for triggers and webtriggers, enabling these triggers to receive input data through IN parameters. As with operations, only scalar data types are supported to ensure consistent and reliable data handling. For more information, see mapTrigger . Enhancement: Access Restrictions for System Triggers in Uniface Applications System triggers are no longer accessible through Public Web access. As a result, assigning the public web access level to a system trigger is no longer permitted. The accept and quit triggers are now classified as Interactive Triggers ; they were previously listed as System Triggers . For more information, see System Triggers , Interactive Triggers , and mapTrigger . 10.4 .03.017 Enhancement: A new label-position property has been introduced for the uxswitch and uxcheckbox UX Widgets. You can now specify label-position as 'before' or 'after' for uxswitch and uxcheckbox widgets. Previously, these widgets only supported a single default label position—'after' for uxcheckbox and 'before' for uxswitch. With this update, the label-position property allows greater flexibility in positioning the label for both widgets. For more details, see uxcheckbox and uxswitch . 10.4 .03.015 Feature: A new field-level widget uxlistbox is added. The uxlistbox widget is a newly introduced field-level widget in the UX widgets set. It serves as a control element that displays a list of items and allows the user to select only single option from a short list. For more information, see uxlistbox and ux-ListBox . Feature: UX Widget IncludeScript The UX Widget is enhanced to support the IncludeScript , which defines fragments of ProcScript or JavaScript code specific to the widget. The physical definition of a UX Widget can specify new properties that refer to the IncludeScript , which is then included in the widget’s script or declarations container. This allows you to define widget-specific code and definitions in one place, making your application more consistent and easier to maintain. For more information, see UX Widget IncludeScript . Feature: UX Widget Interface Version The UX Widget Interface is updated to a new version - Version 2, which introduces a new format for the data argument in the UX Widget Interface API functions: processLayout , onConnect , dataUpdate , and dataCleanup . This new format simplifies the implementation of complex widgets with sub-widgets, enhances flexibility, and streamlines the overall development process. Interface Version 2 is now the default Interface version, and this update introduces breaking changes, making it incompatible with the previous version. As a result, the web.ini file is updated, and the physical widget definition section of a UX widget now includes the uxInterfaceVersion = 2 setting. A widget without this setting, or one created using the previous Interface version, results in a runtime error. To ease the transition, a compatibility utility is available that allows you to make your custom widgets, which were created with the previous Interface version, compatible with Interface Version 2. For more information, see UX Widget Interface Version 2 and Compatibility Utility . 10.4 .03.011 New setting: $LENGTH_RAW_IN_BYTES A new ASN setting $LENGTH_RAW_IN_BYTES is added. Function $length returns either the character length or byte length of raw data based on this new setting. For more information, see $length , $LENGTH_RAW_IN_BYTES , and $random . Discontinued support: Syntax Checking in Static Server Pages The underlying technology, which uses Java Applet, is no longer supported by the browsers. 10.4 .03.010 Feature: Modification dates are re-introduced in the IDE for sub Uniface Development Objects (UDO) Modification dates are re-introduced in the IDE for sub-udos. This feature helps you in implementing better control over the objects you need to re-compile. The compiler sub-switches /aft and /bef can be used again accordingly. For more details, see Uniface 10: Repository Changes per Entity , Uniface Repository , and Uniface Development Objects . 10.4 .03.006 Doc update: Added all Uniface e-learning videos and courses links in the doc library All Uniface e-learning videos, shorts, and courses links are added in the respective topics in Uniface library. For example, see Regex Strings for Pattern Matching and Activate Sentinel Licenses for the Community Edition . 10.4 .03.004 Feature: Added Text Highlighting Feature in Uniface Code Editor Text highlighting has been introduced in the Uniface code editor to help users quickly identify relevant text and tokens. This feature automatically highlights all instances of the selected or caret-based text or token. By default, matching is case-insensitive. For more information, see Text Highlighting . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
